Mitheithel; its waters are brown, strong, deep, and powerful. For centuries
wayfarers plied this river in boats laden with grain and fruits for the dwarven
city of Khazad-dum; with logs for the building of the great Numenorean fleets,
longer ago, with the works of the elven-smiths of Eregion. Now there is no one
between here and the mountains to buy or sell; the forests have been cut, and
the fields abandoned.
name: Glanduin River

desc:
The great river flows westward here, joined by the inconstant waters of the
Forglanduin coming from the east and north. The valley of the river is flat and
moist, fertilized by soil washed from upstream; but the uneven land round about,
once covered by great and deep forests, is now rocky and soilless, and covered
with treeless brush and heath. A ruined jetty extends from the north bank.
name: Glanduin River

desc:
The channel of the Glanduin meanders through a flat floodplain, a land of reeds,
shallow water, mud flats and sand bars. But here in the main channel the water
is always deep and strong, nourished by the snow that falls in the Hithaeglir to
the east, deep brown with the soil and ash of Eregion that wash off its
once-forested hills.
name: Glanduin River

desc:
The Glanduin flows westward here, parallel to the channel of the Forglanduin
which lies to the north. To the south is fertile land nourished by the rivers'
annual floods. At one time the Glanduin may have been a well-travelled waterway,
but there is nothing along the banks to indicate that people have ever passed
this way.
name: Glanduin River

desc:
The broad Glanduin meanders north and west here through a flat floodplain
surrounding its meeting with the Forglanduin. Its waters are brown and deep, and
its flow is powerful. Gulls sail overhead looking for fish; along the banks you
may see an otter sliding down a mud bank, or a carp splashing in the shallows.
name: Glanduin River

desc:
The river continues towards it's meeting with the Mitheithel at a steady pace as
if worried about being late but pacing itself for the miles ahead. This section
of the river flows in from the east and bends to the north. The bank along the
northern side of the river is closely followed by a road that is thousands of
years old, and showing the signs of it's millennia of neglect. The southern
bank, which at the moment is more west than south, is lined by trees, but you
catch glimpses of a vast plain beyond them.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
Flowing swiftly to the west from the south, the river Glanduin hurries on its
journey to the west. On the northeast bank runs the road of the smiths, and on
the southwest bank trees overhang the water shading the shallows. The rivers
current threatens to pull you west.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
Here the river turns north as it flows from the east. The swift current passes
over boulders creating eddies and ripples along the water's surface. To the
west, there are trees along the bank and the road of the smiths runs along the
oposite side. South of here, the trees fade into a thorny combination of holly
and other bushes.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
The fast, swirling waters of the Glanduin flows from east to west, bubbling and
gurgling past the holly bushes covering the southern bank. To the north the Road
of the Smiths continues to shadow the course of the river, while past the holly
bushes in the south grassy plains can be seen stretching off into the distance.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
The flowing water of the river sparkles, reflecting the sky in a hundred small
places as the current pulls you to the west. On the south bank you see thick
clumps of holly and to the north the road of the smiths is slowly being eroded
by the river.
name: River Glanduin

desc:
The water of the Forglanduin river which pours down from the north collects here
in a shallow, brown pool draining westward. Around you, birds settle on the
exposed gravel bars and mud flats; here and there are deeper pools, possibly
hiding treacherous quicksand.
name: Forglanduin River

desc:
These dense, thorny bushes end at the river to the west. The brush continuess
right to the bank of the river. The ground here is covered with thin branches
that have, probably poisoned, sharp thorns. Further to the west a road passes
the river with the help of an old, sturdy bridge. To the east the brush continue
into some woods while to the south lies a grassy plain.
name: Brush

desc:
All the trees here seem to be young and widely spread out. Further east the
trees become taller and thicker, and they grow much more dense than here.
Because of this the forest seems to become darker and darker eastwards. A river
flows along the edge of the forest in the north. Westwards the forest fades, and
turn into small, dense and thorny bushes. The young, light forest continues
southwards.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The forest is dark and damp here. The dense leaves of the trees above shut out
virtually all light. On the ground the grass is short and wet. Some mushrooms
grow around the base of the trees, but apart from these and some scattered weeds
nothing much seems to grow here. Further east the forest seems even darker and
more dense than here. Westards the trees are younger, and much less dense. The
forest ends up near a river in the north while to the south it continues dark
and damp.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The trees are very tall and old here, with moss covering them entirely. Every
exit from this place is alike, which makes it hard to decide where to go. The
only exception is to the north where there is a green, mossy valley leading
northwards. All around, the only sight to be seen is that of tall, dark trees,
through which no light seems to pass. There are a lot of rocks and boulders on
the ground.
name: Deep within the Forest

desc:
This part of the forest is full of old and tall trees. The leaves seem to block
out all the light. Moss grows all along the sides of the trees. On the ground
the grass and moss is damp, and nothing but a few mushrooms grow here. No tracks
can be seen on the ground. It would seem that not many people have dared to
venture into these woods. The forest seems to end east of here, while it
continues in any other direction.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The large, old trees cover the area from any light that the sun may provide. The
ground is damp, and hardly anything seems to grow here beside mushrooms and some
scattered weeds. The dark forest continues north and east from here, while to
the west the forest seems to become lighter as the trees are younger and not as
dense. At the southern edge of the forest the area is open, covered by small,
thorny bushes.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
This seems to be the south-eastern end of the forest. It's very dark and damp.
The trees are all old, with long branches filled with leaves. They block out any
light coming from the sun. Westwards the forest fades, and small, thorny bushes
cover the area. To the south the forest turns into a flat area, with virtually
nothing but dirt, sand and scattered grass. Beyond the eastern end of the forest
lies an area filled with long grass.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Young trees spread out over a wide area here. Green, short grass grows in
between the trees, and makes the ground soft and comfortable. Eastwards the
trees grow denser and denser, and the forest becomes dark and damp. The young
forest continues northwards. To the south and west the forest fades and large
grassy plains, filled with tall grass, cover the area.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The plains are very flat and they continue westwards. All over the plains long,
uniform grass grows. The grass is dense and thick which makes it hard to notice
small animals living in the grass. Some scattered trees grow into a light forest
northwards. To the south-east a large, old tree appears in the otherwise flat
area. Southwards the grass becomes less and less dense, and the area is nothing
but flat land, consisting of dirt, sand and some grass scattered around.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Dense, thorny bushes grow here. The bushes are low and they create a carpet of
thorns as their branches spread out all over the ground. These brushy areas fade
southwards, and turn into a flat, wide area. To the west the thorny bushes
become scattered, and a grassy plain appears. North and east from here lies a
forest with large, old trees that block the sun.
name: Brush

desc:
The knee-high, dense grass covers the whole area, from the north and eastwards.
To the west there is a small stream running northwards, with a path running by
its eastern bank. Further south the area is all flat, and in the distance there
is a beautiful lake. These plains continue north and eastwards, and further
north-east lies a forest.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The plains are covered with knee-high, dense grass. To the south and west these
grassy plains continue, while to the east the plains end up in some light woods.
Over the trees, far to the east, lies some high mountains with their tops nearly
invisible due to the mist. Northwards the plains are covered by bushes, with
slowly increasing density.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
These plains are filled with medium long, green grass. A few trees are scattered
about at the eastern parts of the plain. West of the plains a road bends from
north to west. At the northern end a river flows from the east, and disappears
beneath a bridge north-west of here. Southwards a path winds its way along a
small stream. The plains continue eastwards leading into a distant forest.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The slope is filled with niches and little irregularities, and what is most
noticeable about the area is the sheer greenery that can be seen all around.
Tall weeping willow trees shelter the ground from the open sky. Green moss
carpets the smooth rocks resting on the ground, which is covered by lush grasses
and clover. The ground, itself, is soft from moisture, which is in full supply
and collects in small pools on the ground.
name: Mossy Valley

desc:
Willows and alders grow thickly here in the fertile soil by the River Glanduin.
Tall reeds grow in the muddy shallows, and you can occasionally hear a splash,
either of a fish or of something trying to catch it.
name: Willow Grove

desc:
The forest around you is dead silent and very little light filters in through
the canopy of the trees above. There is a slightly ominous feel here, but you
can see nothing specifically alarming about the place. To the west the forest
continues and you can just barely make out the form of a large boulder among the
trees. To the south and east the forest gets less gloomy, but to the north you
really can't pick out any details.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
As you enter this wooded area, you notice that this is just the beginning of a
much older and deeper forest, perhaps a section of the great forest that once
covered much of this area in times now mostly forgotten. As you gaze north and
west you realise that the forest is darker and more forbidding. In addition to
being older and deeper, maybe it wouldn't be such a bad idea to head out onto
the plains to the south and east.
name: The Outskirts of a Dark Forest

desc:
Swaying to and fro, the grass around you shows the motion of the wind much like
the ripples on water. Gazing to the south, east and west the grasslands continue
as far as the eye can see. To the north and west is the edge of a dark and
forbidding forest.
name: Grassland

desc:
The plains roll away in all directions, the tall stalks of grass swaying gently
as if in a dance. Far to the north-east a small copse of trees stands out
against the monotony, while north and west of here the grass gradually gives way
to a large forest, several miles across.
name: Grassland

desc:
The flat land continues to the west and south from here. The area consists of
nothing but dirt and sand, with some scattered grass in between. To the
south-west lies a very large tree. The tree appears to be quite menacing in
size, with its long branches casting shadows over the base of the tree. East
from here the grass becomes less scarce.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The land here is very flat and wide. Not much seems to grow here, as the ground
mostly consists of dirt and some sand with some grass scattered around. This
flat land seems to encircle a very large tree to the south. The tree is old,
with dry bark and some cracks running along its side. North from here the area
is covered by dense, thorny bushes, while the flat land continues to the east
and west.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The land here is very flat and wide. Some grass grows scattered around, while
most of the area consists of dirt and sand. Not much seems to grow here, besides
a few old, dried out, bushes that all seem dead. This flat land seems to
encircle a very large tree to the south-east. The flat lands continue east and
westwards. To the north the grass become denser, and a large grassy plain
appears.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The land here is flat and wide. It stretches out east and south, covered in
long, uniform grass. The flat area seems to encircle a very large tree to the
southeast. To the north you can see some grassy plains stretching eastwards. To
the west there is a small path following the course of a stream which runs
northwards, leading from a lake which you can just glimpse far in the south.
name: Flat Land

desc:
You have arrived at the ruins of a group of buildings which were built against a
low bluff to the north. They are very old indeed and for the most part only the
foundations remain, completely overgrown with brush and creepers. The walls and
ceilings may have been made of wood, as they have completely vanished. An
inscribed block survives, which may be the cornerstone of the main structure.
name: Ruins

desc:
A fell silence pervades this dark, sunken courtyard. You tread upon ancient
ashes. A smell of burned meat and fat comes up from the ground; charred bones
lie about. Yet this is no recent pyre. The air is heavy with timeless sorrow and
rage, and a red flickering light seems to bathe the sunken courtyard. The walls
are steep, and scorched black.
name: Ash-filled Courtyard

desc:
A few birds and animals seem to be about their business here, but they seem
furtive, as if there was something dangerous nearby. It could be your
imagination, but to the north and west the trees seem bigger and the interior of
the forest is unusually dark. Maybe the holly scrub to the east or the
grasslands south of here would be safer.
name: The Outskirts of a Dark Forest

desc:
Here, close to the Glanduin, its annual floods have built up a layer of fertile
soil. Willows, alders, and leafy bushes grow thickly here. There is no sign that
anyone has lived here for hundreds of years, although here and there you see a
stone that might once have been part of the foundation of a house or barn. War
and plague have left little trace of the farms of Cardolan.
name: Willow Grove

desc:
You are in a wetland, where muddy ground covered with tall reeds slopes
imperceptibly northward and eastward into the shallows of the Glanduin. The
great river loops westward here, and when the water is high this low ground must
all be completely submerged.
name: Marshy Ground

desc:
Bushes and thorns mixed with holly pull at your clothing every time you try to
move here. To the north and west you see the edge of some forests, though the
west looks a bit more gloomy. To the east there is more holly scrub before a
thin strip of trees. All points south however are open grassland stretching to
the horizon, with the exception of a small copse of trees standing by itself.
name: Holly Scrub

desc:
It is really hard to move here on account of the thick bushes and holly laced
with thorns. To the west the way is similar, though you can see that the line of
the trees to your north eventually curves around to the south in the distance.
The sound of flowing water lapping a bank reaches you through the trees to the
east. To the south, the endless plains of the Enedwaith stretch as far as the
eye can see, interrupted only by a small copse of trees which stands alone to
the southwest.
name: Holly Scrub

desc:
A stand of trees has taken advantage of the sustenance provided by the Glanduin
and thrives on this bank of the river. Beyond this narrow fringe, the prairie
again dominates the landscape -- only along the river is there enough moisture
to support such life.
name: Among the Trees

desc:
A mix of holly, thistle and thorns grow here making your going somewhat
difficult and leaving hundreds of little red scratches on any exposed skin below
about three feet. The way looks easier among the trees to the north and east or
in the tall grasses to the south and west.
name: Holly Scrub

desc:
The trees here sway gently in the breeze and whisper softly to the wind. To the
north and east, the river Glanduin flows westward quickly and quietly. The
thorny looking scrub to the south and west is rather uninviting but there
appears to be open grassland beyond that.
name: Among the Trees

desc:
The grass here is about four feet high and your ankles are getting slightly wet
from the water that has not yet evaporated at the base of the grass. Down near
the roots there is almost no breeze and the sun is blocked by the grass stems.
To the north and east is holly scrub and to the south and west the grasslands
stretch away in the distance. To the southwest is a small group of trees that
seems to stand alone in the middle of the grass.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Small insects jump away as you wade through the grass here. To the north the
grass gives way to a scrubby combination of holly and other bushes. To the east
and west the grass gets taller and to the south is a stand of trees that seems
to stand alone as if an oasis in the middle of this flat grassy land.
name: Grassland

desc:
The tall grass plays around you swaying and bopping against you in the wind. To
the north and west you see trees looming up, apparently the beginning of a
substantial forest, and to the south and east the grass gets somewhat shorter,
only about three feet tall instead of the four to five foot stalks found here.
To the southeast there appears to be a small copse of trees in the distance,
standing on its own in a sea of grass.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The green sea of grass to the south and west that is the Enedwaith stretches as
far as the eye can see. There is a small island of trees standing to the east.
To the north and northwest the edge of a forest is barely apparent on the
horizon.
name: Grassland

desc:
A cool quiet pervades this small copse of trees, giving the impression that this
simple place is far more significant than it looks. The area has a certain aura
about it. The trees, plants and flowers growing naturally here are no different
from all the others throughout Arda, yet their beauty far exceeds that of any
grove cared for by men or elves.
name: Inside the Small Copse of Trees

desc:
Waist high grass is all around you waving in a gentle to and fro movement. To
the north and east the grass becomes somewhat taller and thicker. To the west, a
small stand of trees rises up out of the plains. South of here you see nothing
but empty grasslands stretching for miles.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass is so tall here that you can barely see over the top of it! You should
be on your guard since almost anything could hide around here. To the north and
east is a prickly looking holly scrub. To the south and west the grass becomes a
bit shorter. Far to the south you can see a huge, solitary tree and there
appears to be a small copse of trees near the horizon to the west.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Prickly and painful, this brushy area is filled with holly, thistle and a long
almost vine like plant with round green leaves and very sharp thorns. This is a
most unpleasant place to be. To the east, the flora is only slightly friendlier,
dominated by holly with fewer thorns. To the north is a wooded area consisting
of relatively young trees. West and south of here is grass so tall that it is
hard to see over.
name: Holly Scrub

desc:
Here on the south bank of the river Glanduin you are surrounded by thick clumps
of holly bushes. Despite the beauty of all those shiny green leaves and bright
berries, the pointed tips of the leaves are sharp and quite painful to the
touch. To the north, the river flows quietly. East and west of here also appears
to be dominated by hollies, but to the south very tall grass takes over the
landscape.
name: Holly along the Bank

desc:
The grass here is amazingly tall. You can only barely make out what might lie in
each direction, and certainly anything or anyone could be hiding just a few
paces away. Unfortunately, this grass seems to harbor several types of insects
whose annoyance factor is inversely proportional to their size.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Tall, thick grass surrounds you, about waist high, and extends in every
direction. The land is very flat, and it seems almost impossible for any place
to be this uniform. There is one break in the monotony however - a lone, dead,
leafless and barkless tree to the east.
name: Grassland

desc:
Dry stalks of grass tickle you as you walk through the waist high grass here.
The landscape is almost entirely flat allowing you to see for great distances in
all directions. You notice an old snag standing all alone to the northeast.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass here is tall and dry coming nearly to your waist. Oddly enough, to the
south and east the grass is both shorter and greener. Other than the colour and
height of the grass there are no visible landmarks.
name: Grassland

desc:
The vast expanse of the Enedwaith spreads out all around you, the grass
whispering softly in the wind, dancing to its own music. Small insects can be
heard chirping in the grass, otherwise the fauna is quiet. In all directions all
that can be seen is green grass, with no prominent features to mark the
landscape.
name: Grassland

desc:
Dry stalks of grass scratch against your legs as you force your way along. The
landscape all around is almost entirely flat allowing you to see for great
distances in all directions, not that there is much to see. To the northwest a
massive tree stands all alone, reaching into the sky, while in all other
directions the major feature is grass.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass here is a mixture of green and brown, apparently in the process of
drying out. You can hear the occasional cricket chirping, and the occasional
call of a meadowlark or nightingale. Other than the lone tree to the west, you
can see nothing but acres of grass, green to the north and brown to the south.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass here is tall and coarse, scratching and exposed skin as you attempt to
force your way through it. Swaying slightly to and fro, it quickly covers the
tracks of your passage, making the grass a good place in which to hide. To the
north the grass gives way to a dense scattering of holly bushes, while to the
south and west the grass thins out and becomes shorter.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Rippling before the wind, the grass around you is about waist high, much shorter
than it is to the north and east, but otherwise much the same. The land is so
flat that it seems like you can see to the edge of the world. One massive tree
with high spreading branches is the only thing taller than you.
name: Grassland

desc:
Tall and magnificent this tree has a trunk that is so thick two men couldn't
wrap their arms around it. The branches spread out many feet from the tree
providing ample shade. Unfortunately there are no branches low enough to reach
for climbing. In all directions you can see nothing except green, grassy plains
rolling on towards the horizon.
name: A Lone Tree

desc:
Grass and small purplish flowers dominate the landscape here. Small insects dart
between flowers gathering nectar and pollen. A closer look reveals that the tips
of the grass have begun to dry out and turn brown. To the north, there is a
large, lonely tree standing all alone in the vast grasslands.
name: Grassland

desc:
The land becomes drier to the southwest -- the green seen to the northeast is
gradually replaced by amber. The slightest whisper of wind causes the entire
landscape to rustle as the drying stalks sway in unison.
name: Drying Grass

desc:
The tall grass all around you seems to have reduced visibility to near zero, and
it it is nearly impossible to tell what might lie in wait here. The small biting
insects that live in these grasses, however, seem to have abolutely no trouble
finding you.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Though the holly lining this side of the bank is quite pretty, you have to be
quite careful to keep from getting stuck by the sharp points on its leaves. To
the north and east, the Glanduin flows by the bank. To the south lies very tall
grass. West of here the vegetation is very similar. Further to the north you can
see that the river is begining to undercut an ancient road.
name: Holly along the Bank

desc:
Holly bushes line the river bank here. Thick and prickly, these plants make
travel quite painful, but not impossible. East and west of here the vegetation
is similar. To the south tall grass takes over and to the north the river
Glanduin flows quietly westward.
name: Holly along the Bank

desc:
The ground here is moist and the grass seems like it is growing out of control.
You can only just barely see over it. If you stand on the tips of your toes you
can tell that there is some sort of lone, dead tree to the south.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Here stands a tall, dead tree with no bark. The only signs of life it bears now
are the holes drilled in its side by flickers and other woodpeckers in their
search for grubs. The only thing along the horizon in all directions is endless
flat grasslands, the Enedwaith.
name: By an Old Snag

desc:
Little voles and mice seem to be common here and are continually scurrying past
your feet. They appear to have built their own little network of tunnels in the
grass. To the north, you see an old dead barkless tree. To the south and east
the grass gets shorter and drier.
name: Grassland

desc:
Short, dry grasses interspersed with thistle and burdock are all around you
here. You also notice bare spots with dried out rocky soil here and there. To
the east there is a large outcropping of rock that seems to jut out of the
ground.
name: Short Grass

desc:
The grass is shorter here and no longer covers every inch of the ground. Through
the breaks you see a stony, dusty, dry soil clearly in need of a good
rainshower. Small weeds and the occasional hardy thistle also grow here. To the
south you can see a large outcrop of rock.
name: Short Grass

desc:
A massive jagged finger of rock rises out of the ground here. Smaller boulders
surround its base. The south side seems to be seeping water from a small spring.
As the water flows south it seems to spread out and seep into the thirsty soil.
As you would expect, the grass is notably greener to the south.
name: A Rocky Outcropping

desc:
The grass here seems to be taller to the north and shorter to the south. This is
apparently the result of water availability since it also changes from green to
brown along the same gradient. Other than grass, the only thing you can see is
an old dead snag to the west and the land seems to rise slightly to the south.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The grass here has gone to seed, the tops have completely dried out, and the
occasional animal or bird can be seen gathering seeds from the large tufts on
top of each stalk. Only a little green is left here. To the south there is even
less. To the north the grass still has some green to it.
name: Dry Grass

desc:
The grass here once grew thickly, and is tall, but now the leaves have turned
brown at the top, and the stalks are releasing their seed, as they dry. Further
south and west the grass is even dryer, while north and east it is still green,
and growing.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass here is about waist high, but it has dried out and turned a light
yellow color. The stalks are dry and brittle, conditions that are just right for
a grassfire. As you look along the horizon you notice a very large tree standing
all by itself to the east.
name: Grassland

desc:
The golden fields of swaying grass extend for miles in every direction from
here, only interrupted by one small copse of trees off to the north west. The
vast extent of the plains of the Enedwaith begins to weigh on your mind, but for
some reason your attention is continually drawn back to those trees.
name: Grassland

desc:
The mixed brown and green grass here is clearly of two types. One has a tufted
head like wheat and the other has a rounded head, and is probably timothy. To
the north is a small stand of trees and bushes growing thickly together. The
view in all other directions is uninterrupted, endless fields of grass.
name: Grassland

desc:
Golden, dry grass waves back and forth around your waist with a gentle swishing
sound. The gently undulating landscape is uninterrupted in all directions.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass here is a mixture of brown and green. The brown tops of the seed
stalks are releasing their seeds at the slightest touch, they are getting into
all your clothing, making you itch. To the south and west the grass is about the
same, but to the north and east it is greener.
name: Drying Grass

desc:
Although these plains are home to hundreds of varieties of grasses, the lack of
moisture means that the stalks are often dry and brittle. To the south, the
blackened remnants of a once mighty tree reach toward the sky like a withered
claw.
name: Drying Grass

desc:
Crunchy, dry grass surrounds you. This area is badly in need of a long soaking
rain. Even cricket calls and vole tunnels through the grass are scarce. The
landscape is much the same in all directions, tinder-dry and featureless.
name: Dry Grass

desc:
The grass here is almost dead. Only the bottoms of the stalks have any green
left in them at all. Tracks and trails of large animals can be seen where the
grass is pushed down and flattened. Some appear to be made by hooves, others are
less distinct.
name: Drying Grass

desc:
The grasslands around you stretch on towards all horizons. There is nothing
remarkable in any direction. Aside from the occasional bird song, things seem
pretty quiet.
name: Grassland

desc:
Tall golden brown stalks of grass sway in the breeze here and there and
essentially everywhere for as far as the eye can see. The lone exception to this
is a small copse of trees to the northeast. Something about those trees feels
different, but you can't be sure what from this distance.
name: Grassland

desc:
Vast endless stretches of grassy plain roll gently away in all directions. Far
in the distance to the west you see a tree standing alone on the plain. To be
visible at this distance it must be bigger than any tree for many miles around.
name: Grassland

desc:
The grass around you appears to be dry and brittle from lack of water. It
crunches as you walk through it and you can see your backtrail clearly. The
horizon is flat and featureless in all directions.
name: Grassland

desc:
Dry, brown grass ripples in the wind around you swaying too and fro. The plains
are brittle and dry for as far as you can see in all directions. To the west the
grassland starts to become rockier, the ground studded with stones and cracks
where the bare earth has dried up.
name: Grassland

desc:
The area is covered with rocks and scattered boulders. Between the rocks and
boulders the ground consists of dirt and short grass, making it an ideal place
for small animals like rabbits and foxes. Small, scattered holes can be seen
around on the ground. To the north a flat area opens up, less rocky but even
more deserted than here. Further west some cliffs rise above the ground and
block the passage and view.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
Northwest from here a large tree grows, with its enormous branches casting long
shadows around its base. Apart from the tree nothing much else seems to grow in
this flat area. The area is covered with dirt and sand, with some scattered
grass inbetween. To the east the lank grass is less scarce and turns into tall,
green grass while the flat area continues north and west from here.
name: Flat Land

desc:
This flat area consists mostly of dirt and sand, besides some scattered grass.
To the west a large tree blocks passage. Its branches span out wide, and cast
long shadows around the base of the tree. The flat, wide open areas continue
south and westwards, while eastward lies an area with tall grass. In the eastern
horizon high mountains rise above the ground, covered with mist.
name: Flat Land

desc:
Huge on the outside, the tree is totally hollow from the inside. The walls of
the inner side of the tree are remarkably smooth. Great care must have been
taken at carving the inside of the tree. Although it appears to be abandoned
now, some noise appears to be coming from below. The bottom of the tree, as well
as the northern wall, has some cracks in it, probably made by ants.
name: Hollow

desc:
The flat land continues to the east and west from here. The area consists mostly
of dirt and sand, with some grass growing scattered in between. To the north
lies a very large tree that blocks the view and passage. The tree appears to be
quite menacing in size, with branches that span out, casting long shadows near
its base. To the south some cliffs block the way.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The cave is dry and warm. It has clearly not been formed by nature, but made by
people long ago. Ancient the cave has been left abandoned for a long time. The
walls are made of huge blocks of stone. Some cracks can be seen on the western
wall. It's a long way up to the roof. Far up on the eastern wall there seems to
be a large crack that lets in some air.
name: Hollow Cave

desc:
Some grass is spread out in between the rocks. The cliff consist of rocky
ground, with some boulders and smaller stones lying around. To the east a wall
of stone blocks the passage. Some cracks can be seen in the wall, as it seems
old and worn out by the weather. Some of the cracks are large enough for small
animals to enter. West and northwards the cliffs fade out, and a large flat area
appears, while to the south a path runs along the cliffs.
name: Cliffs

desc:
The flat lands continue to the east and west from here. To the northwest of you
there is a very large tree growing. The tree appears to be quite menacing in
size. Never before have you encountered such a tree. The branches of the tree
span out, shading a large area at its base. To the south you can see a path
climbing up the hills.
name: Flat Land

desc:
This flat land stretches out in every direction. Besides some scattered grass
and a few dried out bushes in between the sand and dirt, nothing much grows
here. This flat area seems to encircle a very large tree to the east. The tree
blocks the passage in that direction with its long, thick branches. Far to the
north some grassy plains appear, while further south the flat area ends up in
several cliffs rising above the ground.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The beautiful lake spreads out to the south and west, calm and peaceful. The
water here is light blue, and warm. It is possible to see the bottom of the lake
if one looks closely. North from here a steep hill rises from the shore of the
lake and blocks any escape in that direction. To the east a flat area seems to
go on forever.
name: Beautiful Lake

desc:
This is the western end of the beautiful lake. The lake continues east and south
from here. Around the lake flowers grow close to the shore, together with short,
green grass. Beyond the western shore of the lake a group of tall cliffs rise
into the air. They seem impossible to access from here, with the steep slopes
leading up to the top. A small stream flows north away from the lake.
name: Beautiful Lake

desc:
This area is covered with short grass that becomes more sparse in the northern
parts. To the east the ground increasingly consists of rocks as it approaches
some high cliffs rising in the distance. To the west of here a beautiful lake
appears, with different kinds of plants and flowers growing in the short, soft
grass on the shores. The flat lands continue southwards along the edge of the
lake.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The flat land continues to the north and south from here. The area is covered
with scattered grass, growing in between the spots that are covered with sand.
To the north-east there is a very large tree growing. The tree appears to be
quite menacing in size. The branches of the tree span out, casting shadows over
a large area at its base. West from here a large hill rises far above the
ground.
name: Flat Land

desc:
The area here is very grassy, but in some places there are some rocks lying
scattered around within the grass. They have probably been broken off the cliffs
nearby. A tall, old and very large tree grows to the east. The branches of the
tree cast shadows all around its base. To the north and south the flat lands
continue, while in the west a small path runs along the bank of a small stream.
name: Flat Land

desc:
A large hill looms above. There is a small path leading upwards to the top. The
path is very steep, and it seems impossible to proceed in that direction without
any knowledge of climbing. West from here a stream flows by, leading into a lake
to the south. Northwards a path starts and continues along the bank of the
stream. Past the hill eastwards there is nothing but wide flat plains as far as
the eye can see.
name: Foot of the Hill

desc:
To the south lies a small but beautiful lake. The stream flows out from the lake
and heads northwards. Westwards tall cliffs rise up from the side of the stream.
The cliffs seem impossible to access from here, as they are steep and slippery.
East of the stream a steep hill rises into the air. The sides of the hill are
filled with fairly short grass.
name: Mouth of the Stream

desc:
The cliffs are hard and slippery, which makes it hard to move. They got a
beautiful white colour. To the east, far below, a small stream leading into a
lake appear. It seems hard to continue east from here, down the steep side of
the cliffs. Plains reach out to the west, covered with tall, green grass. On the
southern side a path starts, leading down from the cliffs.
name: Cliffs

desc:
The path winds its way down the cliffs to the south and gradually up the cliffs
to the north. The ground is hard and slippery, something that requires full
concentration to avoid falling. There are a lot of big boulders and rocks that
keep diverting the path from its intended straight line. Along the east side of
the path lies a beautiful lake, while to the west plains reach out.
name: A Small Path

desc:
A stream running north and east from here surrounds the cliffs. Further north
lies a large, dark blue lake. Its hard to move here, as the ground is hard,
rocky and hardly ever flat. The cliffs continue south from here, while to the
west a small forest starts at the base of the cliff. The forest is filled with
thin, young pine trees, and beyond it green plains stretch as far as the eye can
see.
name: Cliffs

desc:
The stream continues east and north from here. The water is cold, and the
current makes it difficult to move. To the north the stream passes a bridge,
while on the western bank thorny bushes grow close to the water. The dense
bushes, full of thorns, make it impossible to climb out of the water without
being hurt. South from here high cliffs rise up from the banks of the stream,
and into the air.
name: Small Stream

desc:
The stream flows from the south to the west. It's shallow, and the bottom is
covered with sand, except for a few stones that are spread out on the bottom.
The water is cold, and a small current makes it harder than normal to move.
Beyond the northern bank of the stream a road runs north and west past the
stream with the help of a bridge. East from here a small path leads up to the
side of the road.
name: Small Stream

desc:
The small stream passes east of some tall, white cliffs. It seems impossible to
access the cliffs from here, due to steep walls leading up to the top. A path
follows the stream on the eastern side, apparently leading to a road that lies
north of here. To the south the stream exits into a beautiful lake, while it
continues to the north.
name: Small Stream

desc:
The small passage below the bridge is damp and cold. Pieces of wood and timber
brought here by the stream lie stranded along the banks. The bridge is made of
stone and wood, and a hatch can be seen up between the archways. The water is
shallow, but a strong current makes it difficult to move. The stream continues
southwards, while to the north a large, wide lake continues as far as the eye
can see.
name: Under the Bridge

desc:
From beneath the bridge looks like it's made of stone and wood. It appears as an
old, sturdy bridge, with plants growing on its walls. On one of the walls a
ladder is running up to the archway of the bridge, and a small hatch leads to
the top of the bridge. East of here the river widens and continues northwards,
while to the west a large lake continues as far as the eye can see.
name: Under the Bridge

desc:
The view is amazing from up here, with the possibility of seeing far in every
direction. To the north lies the river, with a road shadowing it along its
length. To the east flat lands stretch into the distance, with the tall peaks of
the Misty Mountains rising into the sky on the horizon. Westwards lies grassy
plains, punctuated by rocky cliffs. Down below, to the south, lies a small
stream with a path running alongside.
name: Top of the Hill

desc:
These plains are filled with long, thick grass that can let a man or animal
disappear with ease. Only movements in the grass can reveal someone trying to
hide. East of here a small path appears in between the tall, thick grass. To the
north-east some tall white cliffs rise high above the green grass, while the
plains continue south and west from here.
name: Plains

desc:
The grass here is long and thick, but still the ground is soft due to short
grass growing in between the long grass. The long grass is tall, and would reach
up to the middle of a normal sized mans stomach. Sounds can be heard, and
movements can be seen as animals move around in the grass. Grassy plains extend
in all directions, as far as the eye can see.
name: Plains

desc:
Plains stretch out in every direction from here, although to the west the plains
are more like fields, with shorter grass and a greater variety of other plants
and flowers. In this area the only thing growing is the long grass which is as
thick as a finger, and so tall that it steals the sunlight from any other plant
trying to live in between the grass
name: Plains

desc:
You are in a large field covered with grass and flowers. Southwards it makes a
slight slope, while east and west it remains rather flat. West of here you can
see a large plain while east of here the plains-like area continues. South of
here the fertile land seems to continue. To the north you can see a dusty plain
that borders a road.
name: In a Field

desc:
Much of the northern area of this field is covered by rubble from the once proud
road that runs both north and west of here. Large rocks and rotten wood lie
scattered about. South and east of here the large and colourful plains continue.
Looking northwards you can see in the distance a tower rising towards the sky.
name: Dusty Plain

desc:
From here the plains stretch out in all directions except north. To the north a
road runs ewst and northwards. Long grass, reaching nearly up to the belly of a
normal sized horse, grows here. Not much else is able to grow in the grass which
dominates the area. The only exceptions are the small patches of rugged plants
and flowers which have forced their way through the grass.
name: Plains

desc:
These plains continue in every direction but north. To the north thick bushes
and brushwood cover the area. The grass here is thick and long. It's hard to
move around in, as it reaches the waist of a man. It's the same type of grass as
can be seen all over these plains. Far east in the horizon some cliffs obscure
the view, as they rise above the tall grass.
name: Plains

desc:
Endless plains can be seen westwards from here. Tall, thick grass dominates
these plains and makes it impossible to see anything on the ground, or to notice
the animals that move around in the grass. North from here a forest covered with
young pine trees begins, while eastwards white cliffs glow in the light as they
rise above the ground.
name: Plains before the Cliffs

desc:
The area is totally covered with thick bushes with long sharp thorns. Any
attempt to move results in an unpleasant and painful experience. Westwards the
bushes seem to be extremely dense, and almost impossible to pass. The bushy area
continues to the north, while eastwards a forest appears. The forest seems to
consist of young pine trees. Beyond the forest to the east some white cliffs
appear in the distant horizon.
name: Brush

desc:
Young pine trees grow here. They are not as tall and thick as the ones often
seen in larger forests. It's possible to see through this forest, as the trees
are not too dense. To the north and west small bushes grow as dense as a wall.
Their thorns don't look too inviting. East beyond the forest white cliffs rise
above the ground, and to the south plains cover the horizon.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The ground is soft here. It is covered by hard earth with thin, brown grass and
high bushes. The bushes grows way over your knees. To the north the bushes
become more scarce and you can see a high building far away. To the south and to
the east the bushes become too dense and entangled for anyone to force their way
through.
name: Swampy Bushes

desc:
The fields grow green all around, some in better shape than others. The soil is
damp, yet not waterlogged as those closer to the river appear to be. The walls
of the city of Tharbad lie to the southwest, beyond the wide waters of the
Greyflood.
name: Rolling Grassland

desc:
You are in the middle of a wide field, filled with short grass. The soil beneath
the grass is fertile and damp, and only just solid enough to give a slight
possibility of walking over it. To the south-west the sound of rushing water
reaches your ears.
name: Rolling Grassland

desc:
This small bit of grassland is not that damp and moist like most of the area
around. The grass continues to the south while the Swanfleet marshes can be seen
to the west. The outline of the path is visible in the north.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
This is a dark, moist corridor of columns under the old Smiths' Road which
curves overhead. Water stands about six inches deep on the deep, soft mud. Your
feet sink in deeply, and when you pull them out a bubble of foul-smelling air
comes up with them.
name: Under the Causeway

desc:
This is a precarious heap of debris under the main span of the old dwarven
causeway. Tree trunks carried down the Forglanduin from who knows where lie
here, tangled with brush and mud; two of the supporting columns have fallen down
here also and lie in sections, and some chunks of stone have collapsed from the
roadway above. Underneath this not-too-solid pile pours the water of the
Forglanduin, making a deep rumbling sound.
name: Debris Pile

desc:
Here, in the black shadows under the ancient causeway, the air is moist and
carries the scent of rotting vegetation and dead animals. Great stone columns
rise around you, fifty feet or more in height. The ground is quite muddy and
cluttered with wet brush and branches. Little things can be heard squirming and
splashing in the mud.
name: Under the Causeway

desc:
A low stone-walled passage leads northward from the river into the levee which
carries the old dwarven road. Its floor is awash with mud, slime, and river
water; soft, scaleless living things brush past your feet.
name: Mouth of a Culvert

desc:
This stone passage formerly carried the waters of a stream through the levee
supporting the elevated road. Something has blocked the culvert at the north
end, however, leaving it dry. Silent, isolated, thirty feet beneath the solid
stones of the ancient pavement, and completely black, the empty passage seems
untenanted except by small animals who have left their tracks here and there in
the dried mud on the floor.
name: In a Culvert

desc:
Here the Forglanduin is a broad, shallow expanse, with exposed gravel bars
running parallel to the east-west current. Clumps of grass have sprung up from
some of the higher mounds of sand. To the north, standing on stone columns forty
feet tall, an ancient causeway carries the Road of the Smiths.
name: Forglanduin River

desc:
Here in the permanent shadow of the ancient causeway is a long dark expanse of
apparently lifeless mud and earth. The smell of decaying leaves fills the
blackness. On the outside of the columns, nearest the light of the rising and
setting sun, the stone is covered with a thin blanket of moss.
name: Under the Causeway

desc:
This ancient stone road follows the outside of a bend in the Glanduin here as it
curves from the south to the west. The road is elevated a few feet above the
surrounding flat, moist terrain here. In the cracks between the stone, grasses
and weeds have begun to sprout. Built five thousand years ago and more, today it
is nearly unused.
name: Road of the Smiths

desc:
Here the ground is moist and fertile; tall grass and leafy bushes grow thickly,
and some great old oak trees stand in clumps. In the daytime songbirds might be
heard twittering in their branches. A small stream trickles out of the rocks to
the east, winding about and emptying into the fens to the north.
name: Oak Grove

desc:
Small bushes eke out a meagre existence on this patch of barren ground. Thorny
vines intertwined among the thin brown branches of the bushes make the thought
of trampling them an undesirable option. The ground is cracked and parched in
some places, a good indication that this area either does not receive much
moisture or that it does not retain it well.
name: Thick Scrub

desc:
Within the deep, thick brush thorny branches lie on the ground. The sharp thorns
make any attempt to move very unpleasant and painful. North of here the Road of
the Smiths continues east and west. Past the road a large lake continues as far
as the eye can see. A light forest, consisting mostly of young pine trees,
appears close to the south while the area west from here is similar to this. A
small stream passes by to the east.
name: Brush

desc:
These sharp thorns from all the bushes makes every move an unpleasant and
painful experience. The small bushes grows very close to each other, and they
more or less create a wall with their long, thorny branches. The bushy area
continues east and southwards, while to the west a road appears. To the south
east lies a small forest, with its young pine trees.
name: Brush

desc:
Chilly water rushes all about in a chaotic and roaring frenzy. From up above,
the muffled sound of falling water vibrates down from the surface. Millions of
air bubbles created by the falling water cloud any view of the surroundings. As
the falling masses of water force you deeper below the surface, another strong
current can be felt rushing at you from the east.
name: In the Rushing Torrent

desc:
The strong current can be felt rushing westward. The water is cold but smooth as
there is little to disturb it along its course here. The diameter of the spring
itself is no more than a few feet as the polished rock sides of the waterway can
be felt all around. To the east the current becomes impossibly difficult.
However, a break near the top of the smooth rock siding of the spring can be
felt and bubbles linger around the edges of the opening.
name: In an Underground Spring

desc:
This opening is quite small and a closer inspection reveals that the walls are
comprised of smooth rock. A few slimy rock extensions hanging down from the
ceiling form primitive beginnings of stalagtites. In the centre of the floor is
a round opening in which water can be seen flowing by. The air is very humid and
cool, yet it becomes more sparse and warm with every breath. It would be
impossible for this small pocket of air to sustain a person for more than a
short period of time.
name: A Subterranean Alcove

desc:
Here the water that has collected in the deep pool to the north drains through a
narrow gap in the debris pile that has collected under the causeway. The waters
flow over a sill of tree trunks, then abruptly plunge down into a churning dark
basin of broken stone before washing you out downstream from the causeway again.
name: A Dark Plunge
